task_completion.description: Log in to Splunk and navigate to the Search app.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk Web interface is visible, logged in as 'admin', and the 'Search & Reporting' app is active.
A dashboard with the title 'IP_Investigation_Tool' is visible in the Splunk UI (either in view mode or edit mode).

task_completion.description: Add a text input with the token 'target_ip' and default value '*'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dashboard edit interface or source XML shows a text input configured with the token 'target_ip' and a default value of '*'.

task_completion.description: Add a dropdown input with the token 'target_index', default value '*', and specific choices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dashboard edit interface or source XML shows a dropdown input configured with the token 'target_index', a default value of '*', and choices for 'security_logs' and 'web_logs'.

task_completion.description: Create a first visualization panel whose query dynamically uses the tokens.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A visualization panel (e.g., timeline chart) is visible, and its search query (visible in edit mode or source) explicitly includes both '$target_index$' and '$target_ip$'.

task_completion.description: Create a second visualization panel whose query dynamically uses the tokens.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second visualization panel (e.g., recent logs table) is visible, and its search query explicitly includes both '$target_index$' and '$target_ip$'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Splunk Web UI to create the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Splunk web interface (http://localhost:8000) being used, rather than terminal commands executing curl requests to the Splunk API.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply write the XML in a local text editor outside of Splunk.
integrity.visual_evidence: Dashboard configuration and XML editing occur within the Splunk Web dashboard editor (UI or Source view), not in an external text editor like nano or gedit.

integrity.description: The dashboard is a genuine Splunk dashboard and not a mocked HTML page.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface displays standard Splunk dashboard elements, such as the Splunk navigation bar, 'Edit' button, 'Export' options, and standard Splunk visualization panels.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dashboard must be named 'IP_Investigation_Tool'. It requires exactly two input tokens: a text input named 'target_ip' and a dropdown named 'target_index'. The dashboard must contain at least 2 visualization panels. Both panels must dynamically reference the tokens in their SPL queries (e.g., using '$target_index$' and '$target_ip$').
